However, bike-sharing also brings some problems: some people park bikes randomly, which blocks the roads; some bikes are damaged or stolen by others. To solve these problems, the government has made rules: bike companies must manage the bikes regularly, and people who damage bikes have to pay for the loss. Now, with better management, bike-sharing has become a better choice for short trips in cities. What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Bike-sharing is convenient and cheap for people's short trips.
options.B: Bike-sharing has problems like random parking and bike damage in cities.
options.C: Chinese cities have solved traffic problems by building bike-sharing systems.
options.D: Bike-sharing in Chinese cities has advantages and problems, and becomes better with standard management.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲共享单车的优势（解决交通问题、方便便宜、环保），再讲问题（乱停、损坏），最后讲政府规范管理后成为更佳短途出行方式。D选项全面覆盖了“优势+问题+管理后变好”的核心，是主旨；A、B选项仅提及优势或问题，属于细节；C选项“解决了交通问题”表述错误，原文只是“解决交通问题的方式之一”，且未提问题和管理，偏离主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Reading is an important skill that helps people learn and grow. For students, reading can improve vocabulary, develop thinking ability and expand knowledge. For adults, reading can reduce stress, enrich life and help them understand the world better. Even for the elderly, reading can keep their brains active and prevent memory loss. Although different people read different types of books (novels, science books, history books, etc.), the benefits of reading are the same for everyone. That's why many countries encourage people to read more books every year. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: Different Types of Books People Like to Read
options.B: The Benefits of Reading for All Age Groups
options.C: How to Improve Reading Skills for Students
options.D: Why Countries Encourage People to Read More Books

exercises.explanation: 原文核心是“阅读对学生、成年人、老年人等不同年龄段人群都有好处，因此多国鼓励阅读”。B选项“阅读对所有年龄段人群的益处”精准概括主旨，且包含核心关键词“reading”和“benefits”；A选项“不同类型的书”是细节，并非核心；C选项“学生提升阅读技巧”仅针对学生，以偏概全；D选项“多国鼓励阅读的原因”只是尾句内容，未覆盖前文“不同人群的益处”，不全面。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: In many countries, food waste has become a serious problem. Every year, about 1.3 billion tons of food is wasted around the world, which is enough to feed 2 billion people. In restaurants, customers often order more food than they can eat, and many of them don't take the leftovers home. At home, people throw away food that is still edible just because it is a little old or not as fresh as before. To reduce food waste, some countries have made laws: restaurants can charge customers for excess food waste, and supermarkets are not allowed to throw away unsold but edible food. Many people also start to take action, like making a shopping list before buying food and eating up all the food on their plates. What is the writer's purpose of writing this passage?

options.A: To introduce the amount of food waste around the world every year.
options.B: To explain why people waste food in restaurants and at home.
options.C: To talk about the problem of food waste and ways to solve it.
options.D: To encourage supermarkets to stop throwing away unsold food.

exercises.explanation: 原文先提出“食物浪费是严重问题”，再用数据说明浪费规模，接着讲餐厅和家庭浪费的原因，最后讲各国法律和人们的行动来减少浪费。C选项“谈论食物浪费问题及解决方法”全面覆盖主旨，符合写作目的；A选项“全球食物浪费量”是细节数据；B选项“浪费原因”仅为原文一部分；D选项“鼓励超市停止丢弃未售出食物”只是解决方法的一个细节，均不能概括写作目的。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Online learning has become more and more popular since the COVID-19 pandemic. It allows students to learn at home at any time, and they can review the lessons as many times as they want. There are also many free online courses from famous teachers, which makes learning more accessible for students in remote areas. However, online learning also has disadvantages: students may get distracted by phones or computer games during classes, and they lack face-to-face communication with teachers and classmates. Some students also have eye problems because they spend too much time looking at screens. Schools and parents are trying to solve these problems: schools make strict online class rules, and parents monitor students' screen time and help them create a quiet learning environment. What does the passage mainly talk about?

options.A: Online learning is popular among students in remote areas.
options.B: Online learning has advantages and disadvantages, and people are solving its problems.
options.C: Students have eye problems because of too much screen time in online learning.
options.D: Schools and parents make rules to help students with online learning.

exercises.explanation: 原文核心结构是“网课流行的优势（随时学、可复习、免费课程）+劣势（分心、缺乏交流、视力问题）+学校和家长的解决措施”。B选项完整概括了这三部分核心内容；A选项仅提“偏远地区学生”，是优势中的细节；C选项仅提“视力问题”，是劣势中的细节；D选项仅提“学校和家长的措施”，是解决方法的细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Animals are important to the earth's ecosystem, but many species are in danger of extinction now. The main reasons are human activities: people cut down forests to build houses and farms, which destroys animals' habitats; people hunt animals for their fur, meat or horns; pollution from factories and cars also makes animals' living environment worse. To protect endangered animals, many countries have set up nature reserves, where animals can live safely. Governments also make laws to stop illegal hunting and deforestation. Many environmental groups also educate people about the importance of protecting animals and encourage them to take part in protection activities. What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Human activities are the main cause of animal extinction.
options.B: Nature reserves help endangered animals live safely.
options.C: Many animal species are in danger because of humans, and people are protecting them.
options.D: Environmental groups educate people to protect endangered animals.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“动物濒危，主要原因是人类活动”，再讲“各国建自然保护区、立法、环保组织宣传教育等保护措施”。C选项全面概括了“濒危原因+保护措施”的核心；A选项仅提原因，B选项仅提自然保护区，D选项仅提环保组织的行动，均为细节，不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Sleep is essential for human health, but many teenagers don't get enough sleep these days. A survey shows that 70% of middle school students sleep less than 8 hours a night. The main reasons are: they have too much homework and stay up late to finish it; some students play mobile phones or watch TV before bedtime, which makes it hard for them to fall asleep; some students have irregular sleep habits, like sleeping late on weekends and getting up early on weekdays. Lack of sleep makes students tired in class, hard to concentrate and easy to get angry. To improve sleep quality, doctors suggest that students should finish homework on time, avoid using electronic devices before bed, and keep a regular sleep schedule every day. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: A Survey About Teenagers' Sleep Time
options.B: Why Teenagers Lack Sleep and How to Improve It
options.C: The Bad Effects of Lack of Sleep on Teenagers
options.D: How to Keep a Regular Sleep Schedule for Students

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“青少年睡眠不足”，再分析原因（作业多、玩手机、作息不规律），接着讲睡眠不足的影响，最后讲医生的改善建议。B选项“青少年睡眠不足的原因及改善方法”精准概括主旨，是最佳标题；A选项“睡眠时长调查”是细节数据；C选项“睡眠不足的影响”仅为原文一部分；D选项“保持规律作息”只是改善方法的一个细节，均不能作为最佳标题。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Volunteering is a meaningful activity that benefits both volunteers and the community. Volunteers can help people in need, like the elderly, the disabled and homeless people. They can also protect the environment by cleaning up parks, planting trees and reducing plastic waste. For volunteers themselves, volunteering can develop their communication skills, build confidence and make new friends. It also makes them more grateful for what they have and understand the society better. Many schools encourage students to take part in volunteering activities, and some even make it a part of the students' comprehensive evaluation. What does the writer want to tell us in this passage?

options.A: Volunteers can help the elderly and disabled people in the community.
options.B: Volunteering activities include environmental protection and helping others.
options.C: Volunteering is meaningful for volunteers and the community, so schools encourage it.
options.D: Volunteering can help students develop communication skills and make friends.

exercises.explanation: 原文首句即主旨句“志愿服务对志愿者和社区都有意义”，接着讲志愿服务的具体形式（帮助他人、环保）、对志愿者的好处，最后讲学校鼓励学生参与。C选项完整概括了“志愿服务的意义+学校的鼓励”；A选项仅提“帮助老人和残疾人”，是志愿服务的一个细节；B选项仅提“志愿服务的形式”，是细节；D选项仅提“对学生的好处”，是志愿者益处的一部分，均不能概括作者想表达的核心。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Mobile phones have changed people's life a lot in the past 20 years. In the past, people used mobile phones only to make calls and send text messages. But now, mobile phones are like small computers: people can use them to surf the Internet, watch videos, shop online, play games and even work. They also make communication easier—people can video call with family and friends far away at any time. However, mobile phones also bring problems: many people spend too much time on phones and ignore face-to-face communication; some students use phones in class, which affects their study; there are also more cyber crimes because of personal information leakage from phones. What is the passage mainly about?

options.A: Mobile phones can be used to surf the Internet and shop online now.
options.B: Mobile phones have changed people's communication ways a lot.
options.C: Mobile phones have greatly changed people's life with advantages and problems.
options.D: Students should not use mobile phones in class to avoid affecting study.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“手机20年来极大改变人们生活”，再对比过去和现在的功能（优势），最后讲手机带来的问题（沉迷、影响学习、信息泄露）。C选项“手机极大改变生活，有优势也有问题”全面概括主旨；A选项“手机的上网和购物功能”是优势中的细节；B选项“改变交流方式”只是优势的一部分；D选项“学生不应上课用手机”是问题中的细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Recycling is an effective way to protect the environment and save resources. It can reduce the amount of waste sent to landfills, and it also saves energy because making new products from recycled materials uses less energy than making them from raw materials. For example, recycling one ton of paper can save 17 trees and 7000 gallons of water. Recycling different materials has different benefits: recycling plastic reduces oil use, recycling metal saves minerals, and recycling glass reduces air pollution. Many cities have set up recycling bins in communities and schools to encourage people to recycle. However, some people still don't recycle because they think it is troublesome or they don't know how to sort waste correctly. Local governments are trying to solve this by teaching people waste sorting skills and making recycling more convenient. What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Recycling one ton of paper can save many trees and water.
options.B: Recycling different materials has different environmental benefits.
options.C: Recycling is good for the environment but faces problems, and governments are solving them.
options.D: Cities set up recycling bins to encourage people to recycle waste.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“回收利用对环保和节约资源有效”，再用例子和不同材料的回收好处支撑，接着讲城市的鼓励措施，最后讲“部分人不回收的问题”及政府的解决方法。C选项完整概括了“回收的好处+存在的问题+政府的解决措施”；A选项是举例细节；B选项是回收好处的一部分；D选项是鼓励措施的细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Sports are not only good for people's physical health but also their mental health. Playing sports can strengthen muscles and bones, improve heart function and reduce the risk of diseases like diabetes and high blood pressure. For mental health, sports can help people reduce stress and anxiety, and improve their mood by releasing endorphins (内啡肽). Team sports like football and basketball also teach people teamwork and communication skills, and help them make more friends. However, it is important to do sports in a proper way: people should warm up before exercising to avoid injuries, and they should not exercise too much or too hard, especially when they are tired or sick. What is the writer's purpose of writing this passage?

options.A: To explain how team sports help people make friends and learn teamwork.
options.B: To tell people the physical and mental benefits of sports and the right way to do sports.
options.C: To introduce the benefits of sports for people's mental health.
options.D: To warn people not to exercise too hard to avoid injuries.

exercises.explanation: 原文核心是“运动对身体和心理健康都有益处，同时要以正确方式运动”。B选项全面概括了写作目的；A选项仅提“团队运动的好处”，是细节；C选项仅提“心理健康益处”，是好处的一部分；D选项仅提“避免过度运动受伤”，是正确运动方式的细节，均不能作为写作目的。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Books are often called "the window to the world" because they can open people's minds and let them know about different cultures, histories and ideas. Fiction books like novels can take readers to different times and places, and make them experience different lives. Non-fiction books like science books and biographies can teach readers practical knowledge and real-life stories of great people. Reading books also helps people improve their language skills, whether it is their mother tongue or a foreign language. In today's digital age, although e-books are popular, many people still like physical books because they enjoy the feeling of holding a book and turning the pages. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: Fiction and Non-fiction Books Have Different Benefits
options.B: E-books Are Popular in the Digital Age
options.C: Books: The Window to the World with Various Benefits
options.D: Reading Books Can Improve People's Language Skills

exercises.explanation: 原文首句点明“书被称为世界之窗，能开阔视野、了解不同文化历史”，接着讲小说和非小说类书籍的好处、阅读对语言能力的提升，最后讲数字时代实体书仍受喜爱。C选项“书籍：有多种益处的世界之窗”既包含核心比喻（世界之窗），又概括了“多种益处”的主旨，是最佳标题；A选项“小说和非小说的好处”是细节；B选项“电子书流行”仅为原文最后一句的部分内容；D选项“提升语言能力”是好处的一个细节，均不能作为最佳标题。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Traveling is a great way to relax and learn new things. When people travel to different places, they can experience different cultures, taste local food and meet new people. Traveling also helps people get out of their daily routine and reduce stress. For students, traveling can be a kind of education outside the classroom—they can learn about history by visiting ancient buildings, and learn about geography by seeing different landscapes like mountains, rivers and deserts. However, traveling also needs careful planning: people should book tickets and hotels in advance, make a travel plan and prepare necessary things like clothes and medicine. They should also respect local customs and protect the environment when traveling. What does the passage mainly talk about?

options.A: Traveling can help students learn history and geography outside the classroom.
options.B: People should plan their trips carefully and respect local customs when traveling.
options.C: Traveling is a good way to relax and learn, and it needs careful planning.
options.D: Traveling allows people to experience different cultures and taste local food.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“旅行是放松和学习新事物的好方式”，再讲对学生的教育意义，最后讲“旅行需要周密计划及注意事项”。C选项全面概括了核心内容；A选项仅提“学生的学习意义”，是细节；B选项仅提“旅行计划和尊重习俗”，是后半部分内容；D选项仅提“体验文化和美食”，是旅行好处的细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Water is one of the most important natural resources, but many people waste it without realizing it. At home, people often leave the tap running while brushing teeth or washing dishes, and they water the plants with too much water. In agriculture, farmers use flood irrigation (漫灌) which wastes a lot of water, even though drip irrigation (滴灌) is more water-saving. In industries, many factories use a large amount of water and discharge polluted water into rivers, which makes clean water less available. To save water, governments promote water-saving technologies like drip irrigation and recycle industrial water. Schools and communities also educate people to form good habits, like turning off taps in time and reusing water (e.g., using water from washing vegetables to water plants). What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Many people waste water at home by leaving taps running or overwatering plants.
options.B: Agriculture and industry waste a lot of water and pollute clean water resources.
options.C: Water is important but widely wasted, and people are taking actions to save it.
options.D: Governments promote water-saving technologies to reduce industrial water waste.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“水是重要资源但被大量浪费”，再分家庭、农业、工业讲浪费方式，最后讲政府、学校和社区的节水措施。C选项完整概括了“水的重要性+浪费现象+节水行动”的核心；A选项仅提“家庭浪费”，B选项仅提“农业和工业浪费”，D选项仅提“政府推广节水技术”，均为细节，不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Friendship is an important part of people's life, and a true friend can bring happiness and support to us. True friends listen to us when we are sad, encourage us when we are in trouble and share our joy when we succeed. They also help us become better people by pointing out our mistakes and giving honest advice. However, building and keeping a true friendship is not easy: we need to be honest and respectful to our friends, keep our promises and spend time with them. We should also learn to forgive their mistakes and communicate with them when there are misunderstandings. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: True Friends Can Help Us Become Better People
options.B: How to Build and Keep a True Friendship
options.C: Friendship Is Important and Needs Efforts to Keep
options.D: True Friends Listen to Us and Share Our Feelings

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“友谊重要，真朋友带来快乐和支持”，再讲“建立和维持真友谊不容易，需要付出努力（诚实、尊重、守诺、沟通等）”。C选项“友谊很重要，且需要努力去维持”全面概括主旨，是最佳标题；A选项仅提“真朋友帮助我们成长”，是友谊重要性的细节；B选项仅提“如何建立维持友谊”，是原文后半部分内容；D选项仅提“真朋友的陪伴”，是友谊重要性的细节，均不能作为最佳标题。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Art education is an important part of school education, but it is often ignored in many schools. Art classes like painting, music and dance can help students develop creativity and imagination, which are important for their future development. They also help students express their feelings and reduce stress from study. Art education can also make students more culturally literate (有文化修养的) by letting them know about different art forms and artists from around the world. However, many schools cut art classes to make more time for math, English and other exam subjects. Some parents also think art is not as important as academic subjects and don't support their children's art learning. To change this situation, education departments are making rules to ensure that schools offer enough art classes, and they are also training more art teachers to improve the quality of art education. What does the passage mainly talk about?

options.A: Art classes help students develop creativity and reduce study stress.
options.B: Many schools cut art classes to make time for exam subjects.
options.C: Art education is important but ignored, and efforts are made to improve it.
options.D: Parents should support their children's learning of art subjects.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“艺术教育重要但被忽视”，再讲艺术教育的好处，接着讲学校和家长忽视艺术教育的现象，最后讲教育部门的改善措施。C选项全面概括了核心内容；A选项仅提“艺术课的好处”，是细节；B选项仅提“学校削减艺术课”，是忽视的表现之一；D选项仅提“家长应支持艺术学习”，是改善措施的一个细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Time management is a key skill for students to succeed in study and life. Good time management can help students finish homework on time, have enough time for rest and hobbies, and avoid last-minute rushing for exams. To manage time well, students should make a daily or weekly plan, list their tasks in order of importance, and focus on one task at a time instead of multitasking. They should also avoid procrastination (拖延), like putting off homework until the night before it's due. However, many students struggle with time management: some spend too much time on phone games or social media, some don't know how to prioritize tasks, and some are easily distracted by their surroundings. Teachers and parents can help by teaching students planning skills, setting clear rules for screen time and creating a quiet study space. What is the writer's purpose of writing this passage?

options.A: To teach students how to make a daily or weekly study plan.
options.B: To explain why many students have problems with time management.
options.C: To tell students the importance of time management and how to improve it.
options.D: To suggest that teachers and parents help students with time management.

exercises.explanation: 原文核心是“时间管理对学生很重要，介绍了做好时间管理的方法，也讲了学生的时间管理问题及老师家长的帮助方式”。C选项“告诉学生时间管理的重要性及如何提升该能力”全面概括写作目的；A选项仅提“制定计划”，是方法的细节；B选项仅提“学生时间管理的问题原因”，是原文一部分；D选项仅提“老师家长的帮助”，是改善方法的一部分，均不能作为写作目的。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Air pollution is a serious global problem that affects people's health and the environment. The main sources of air pollution are factory emissions, vehicle exhaust, burning of coal and wood, and dust from construction sites. Long-term exposure to polluted air can cause respiratory diseases (呼吸道疾病), heart problems and even cancer. It also harms plants and animals, and causes acid rain which damages buildings and soil. Many countries are taking action to reduce air pollution: they make stricter rules for factory emissions and vehicle exhaust, promote clean energy like solar and wind power, and encourage people to use public transport instead of private cars. Some cities also plant more trees to absorb harmful gases and improve air quality. What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Air pollution causes respiratory diseases and heart problems for people.
options.B: Factory emissions and vehicle exhaust are the main sources of air pollution.
options.C: Air pollution is a serious problem, and countries are taking measures to solve it.
options.D: Planting more trees can absorb harmful gases and improve air quality.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“空气污染是严重全球问题，影响健康和环境”，再讲污染源和危害，最后讲各国的减排措施。C选项全面概括了“问题+危害+解决措施”的核心；A选项仅提“对人体的危害”，是危害的一部分；B选项仅提“污染源”，是原文一部分；D选项仅提“种树改善空气质量”，是解决措施的一个细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Learning a foreign language is not only about memorizing words and grammar rules, but also about understanding the culture behind the language. Knowing the culture can help learners communicate better with native speakers and avoid cultural misunderstandings. For example, in Western countries, it is polite to say "thank you" even for small things like getting a glass of water, while in some Asian countries, people may not say it as often. Learning about festivals, food and customs of the target language country also makes learning more interesting and meaningful. However, many language learners only focus on memorizing words and passing exams, and they ignore cultural learning. To solve this problem, language teachers are adding cultural content to their lessons, like watching foreign movies, reading cultural stories and having discussions about different customs. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: How to Avoid Cultural Misunderstandings in Foreign Language Communication
options.B: Foreign Language Learning Should Include Cultural Understanding
options.C: Memorizing Words and Grammar Is Not Enough for Language Learning
options.D: Teachers Add Cultural Content to Foreign Language Lessons

exercises.explanation: 原文核心是“学习外语不仅要记单词语法，还要理解背后的文化，而很多学习者忽视文化学习，老师正加入文化内容改善”。B选项“外语学习应包含文化理解”精准概括主旨，是最佳标题；A选项“避免文化误解”是文化学习的一个作用，细节；C选项“只记单词语法不够”仅为原文观点的一部分；D选项“老师加入文化内容”是解决方法的细节，均不能作为最佳标题。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Pets are more than just animals to many people—they are family members. Keeping a pet can bring many benefits: pets can reduce loneliness, especially for the elderly or people living alone; they can also encourage people to be more active, like walking a dog every day; and playing with pets can improve people's mood and reduce stress. Different pets have different characteristics: dogs are loyal and energetic, cats are independent and quiet, and fish are easy to take care of. However, keeping a pet also requires responsibility: owners need to feed them on time, take them to the vet when they are sick, and spend time with them. Some people abandon their pets when they get tired of them, which causes problems like stray animals and environmental pollution. Animal protection organizations are educating people about responsible pet ownership and rescuing stray pets. What does the passage mainly talk about?

options.A: Different pets have different characteristics and benefits for people.
options.B: Pets are family members and can reduce loneliness for the elderly.
options.C: Keeping pets has benefits but requires responsibility, and organizations are promoting responsible ownership.
options.D: Abandoning pets causes stray animal problems and environmental pollution.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“宠物是家庭成员，养宠物有好处”，再讲不同宠物的特点，接着讲“养宠物需要责任”，最后讲“弃养问题及动物保护组织的行动”。C选项全面概括核心内容；A选项仅提“宠物特点和好处”，是细节；B选项仅提“宠物对老人的作用”，是好处的细节；D选项仅提“弃养的危害”，是原文一部分，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Breakfast is often called "the most important meal of the day", and it is true for both children and adults. Eating a healthy breakfast can provide energy for the whole morning, help people concentrate better at work or school, and prevent overeating at lunch. A healthy breakfast should include different nutrients: whole grains (like bread or oatmeal), protein (like eggs or milk), and fruits or vegetables. However, many people skip breakfast because they get up late, don't have time to cook, or want to lose weight. Skipping breakfast can make people tired, irritable and unable to focus, and it may even lead to weight gain in the long run because of overeating later. To encourage people to eat breakfast, schools and companies are providing breakfast options, and nutritionists are teaching people how to make quick and healthy breakfasts at home. What is the writer's purpose of writing this passage?

options.A: To introduce the nutrients that a healthy breakfast should include.
options.B: To explain why many people skip breakfast every day.
options.C: To tell people breakfast is important, warn against skipping it and suggest solutions.
options.D: To teach people how to make quick and healthy breakfasts at home.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“早餐是最重要的一餐，健康早餐的好处和营养构成”，再讲“很多人不吃早餐的原因及危害”，最后讲“学校、公司和营养学家的鼓励措施”。C选项全面概括写作目的；A选项“健康早餐的营养”是细节；B选项“不吃早餐的原因”是原文一部分；D选项“制作快速健康早餐”是解决方法的细节，均不能作为写作目的。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Forests are vital to the earth's health, as they provide oxygen, absorb carbon dioxide, and are home to millions of animal and plant species. They also help prevent soil erosion (水土流失) and regulate the climate. However, deforestation (砍伐森林) is happening at a fast rate around the world: people cut down forests to get wood, build farms or cities, and this leads to serious problems like global warming, loss of biodiversity and more natural disasters (e.g., floods and droughts). Many international organizations are working to stop deforestation: they support reforestation (重新造林) projects, help local people find alternative ways to make money (instead of cutting trees), and make rules to protect forests. Some countries also set up protected forest areas where cutting trees is not allowed. What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Forests provide oxygen and are home to many animals and plants.
options.B: Deforestation causes global warming and loss of biodiversity.
options.C: Forests are important but being destroyed, and actions are taken to protect them.
options.D: International organizations support reforestation projects to save forests.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“森林对地球健康至关重要”，再讲“砍伐森林的现状和危害”，最后讲“国际组织和各国的保护措施”。C选项完整概括核心内容；A选项仅提“森林的作用”，是细节；B选项仅提“砍伐森林的危害”，是原文一部分；D选项仅提“国际组织的重新造林项目”，是保护措施的细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Manners are important in all cultures, but different cultures have different rules of manners. In some countries, it is polite to shake hands when meeting someone for the first time, while in others, people bow or hug. When eating, some cultures use chopsticks, some use forks and knives, and some eat with their hands. It is also important to know table manners: in Western countries, people don't talk with their mouths full, and they don't put their elbows on the table. In some Asian countries, it is polite to let the elders eat first. However, many people make mistakes in cross-cultural communication because they don't know the manners of other cultures. To avoid this, people can learn about different cultural manners through books, travel or talking to people from other countries. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: Different Table Manners in Western and Asian Countries
options.B: How to Greet People Politely in Different Cultures
options.C: Cultural Differences in Manners and How to Avoid Mistakes
options.D: Manners Are Important in Cross-cultural Communication

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“礼貌在所有文化中都重要，但不同文化有不同礼貌规则”，再举例见面和餐桌礼仪的差异，接着讲“跨文化交流中因不懂礼貌规则犯错”，最后讲“避免错误的方法”。C选项“礼貌的文化差异及如何避免错误”全面概括主旨，是最佳标题；A选项“中西餐桌礼仪”是举例细节；B选项“不同文化的问候方式”是举例细节；D选项“礼貌在跨文化交流中重要”仅为原文开头观点，不全面，均不能作为最佳标题。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Music is a universal language that can connect people from different countries and cultures. It can express emotions like happiness, sadness, anger and love, and people can understand it even without words. Different types of music have different effects: classical music can help people relax and focus, pop music can make people feel energetic, and traditional music can keep cultural heritage alive. Music also plays an important role in people's life: it is used in festivals, celebrations, and even in therapy to help people with mental health problems. However, with the development of digital music, many musicians struggle to make money because of piracy (盗版) and free online music platforms. Some people also listen to music too loudly or at inappropriate times (like in libraries), which disturbs others. Governments and music organizations are making rules to protect musicians' rights and educate people to listen to music politely. What does the passage mainly talk about?

options.A: Different types of music have different effects on people's emotions.
options.B: Music is a universal language that connects people across cultures.
options.C: Music is important but faces problems, and measures are taken to solve them.
options.D: Digital music causes piracy problems and affects musicians' income.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“音乐是通用语言，有不同作用和在生活中的重要性”，再讲“数字音乐带来的盗版问题、音乐播放不当打扰他人”，最后讲“政府和组织的解决措施”。C选项全面概括核心内容；A选项仅提“音乐类型的影响”，是细节；B选项仅提“音乐的通用语言属性”，是开头观点；D选项仅提“数字音乐的盗版问题”，是问题的一部分，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Gardening is a popular hobby for many people, and it has both physical and mental benefits. Physically, gardening involves digging, planting and watering, which is a form of exercise that can improve strength and flexibility. It also allows people to get more fresh air and sunlight, which is good for vitamin D production. Mentally, gardening can reduce stress and anxiety, and it gives people a sense of achievement when they see plants grow and bloom. Growing your own vegetables and fruits also means you can eat fresh and healthy food without pesticides (农药). However, gardening requires patience and knowledge: plants need the right amount of water, sunlight and soil, and they may get sick or be attacked by pests. Beginners often make mistakes like overwatering plants or choosing the wrong plants for their climate. Garden centers and online resources offer advice to help beginners, and experienced gardeners often share their tips with others. What is the writer's purpose of writing this passage?

options.A: To explain why gardening is a popular hobby for many people.
options.B: To tell people the physical and mental benefits of gardening.
options.C: To introduce gardening's benefits, challenges and ways to get help.
options.D: To warn beginners against common mistakes in gardening.

exercises.explanation: 原文核心是“园艺是受欢迎的爱好，有身心益处，但需要耐心和知识，新手易犯错，可通过园艺中心和网络获取帮助”。C选项“介绍园艺的益处、挑战及获取帮助的方式”全面概括写作目的；A选项“园艺受欢迎的原因”仅为原文开头部分；B选项“园艺的身心益处”是原文一部分；D选项“新手的常见错误”是挑战的细节，均不能作为写作目的。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Urban parks are important green spaces in cities, and they provide many benefits for citizens. They offer a place for people to relax, exercise and spend time with family and friends. Parks also improve air quality by absorbing carbon dioxide and releasing oxygen, and they help reduce the urban heat island effect (城市热岛效应). Many parks have playgrounds for children, sports facilities for adults and benches for the elderly, making them suitable for people of all ages. However, some urban parks are facing problems: they are overcrowded on weekends and holidays, some people litter or damage park facilities, and some parks lack maintenance (维护) due to insufficient funding. City governments are taking action: they are building more parks in crowded areas, making stricter rules to protect park facilities, and increasing funding for park maintenance. They also encourage citizens to volunteer to clean parks and take care of plants. What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Urban parks have playgrounds and sports facilities for people of all ages.
options.B: Urban parks are overcrowded and have litter and facility damage problems.
options.C: Urban parks are important green spaces with benefits and problems, and cities are improving them.
options.D: Citizens should volunteer to clean urban parks and protect park facilities.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“城市公园是重要绿地，对市民有诸多益处”，再讲“公园面临拥挤、垃圾、设施损坏、维护资金不足等问题”，最后讲“市政府的改善措施和鼓励市民志愿行动”。C选项全面概括核心内容；A选项仅提“公园的设施”，是益处的细节；B选项仅提“公园的问题”，是原文一部分；D选项仅提“市民志愿行动”，是改善措施的细节，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Memory is a key ability for learning and daily life, and there are many ways to improve it. One effective way is to use active learning methods, like explaining what you've learned to others or making flashcards for key points, instead of just reading and memorizing passively. Getting enough sleep is also important, as sleep helps the brain organize and store memories. Regular exercise can improve blood flow to the brain and boost memory. Eating a healthy diet with fruits, vegetables and nuts also supports brain health. However, many people have bad habits that harm memory: staying up late, eating too much junk food, and relying too much on phones to remember things (like phone numbers or to-do lists). To improve memory, people need to change these bad habits and stick to healthy lifestyle choices. Teachers also teach students memory strategies in class, like chunking (分块记忆) and association (联想记忆) to help them remember knowledge better. What is the best title for the passage?

options.A: Active Learning Methods to Improve Memory
options.B: Healthy Lifestyle Choices for Better Memory
options.C: Ways to Improve Memory and Avoid Bad Habits
options.D: Teachers Teach Memory Strategies to Students

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“记忆重要，有多种改善方法（主动学习、睡眠、运动、饮食）”，再讲“损害记忆的坏习惯”，最后讲“改变坏习惯和老师教记忆策略”。C选项“改善记忆的方法及避免坏习惯”精准概括主旨，是最佳标题；A选项“主动学习方法”是改善方法的一个细节；B选项“健康生活方式”是改善方法的一部分；D选项“老师教记忆策略”是改善方法的一个细节，均不能作为最佳标题。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Cultural heritage (文化遗产) includes ancient buildings, traditional art, folk customs and historical relics, and it is an important part of a country's identity. Protecting cultural heritage can help people understand their history and culture, and pass them on to future generations. Many countries protect cultural heritage by repairing ancient buildings, setting up museums to display relics, and teaching traditional art in schools. However, cultural heritage is facing many threats: natural disasters like earthquakes and floods can damage ancient buildings; urban development may destroy historical sites; and some traditional arts are disappearing because young people are not interested in learning them. To protect cultural heritage, governments are making laws to protect historical sites, and cultural organizations are holding activities to promote traditional art, like folk music concerts and craft workshops. They also encourage young people to learn traditional skills and become inheritors (传承人) of cultural heritage. What does the passage mainly talk about?

options.A: Cultural heritage includes ancient buildings, traditional art and folk customs.
options.B: Natural disasters and urban development threaten cultural heritage.
options.C: Cultural heritage is important and needs protection against various threats.
options.D: Governments and organizations protect cultural heritage with laws and activities.

exercises.explanation: 原文先讲“文化遗产的定义和重要性”，再讲“保护文化遗产的方式”，接着讲“文化遗产面临的威胁”，最后讲“政府和组织的保护措施”。C选项“文化遗产很重要，需要保护以应对各种威胁”全面概括主旨；A选项“文化遗产的构成”是定义细节；B选项“文化遗产的威胁”是原文一部分；D选项“政府和组织的保护措施”是原文一部分，均不能作为主旨。

exercises.question: Read the passage and answer the question: Laughter is often called "the best medicine" because it has many health benefits. Laughing can reduce stress hormones (荷尔蒙) in the body, lower blood pressure and boost the immune system (免疫系统). It also exercises facial muscles and improves lung function. Socially, laughter can strengthen relationships between people, as sharing a laugh with friends or family creates a positive and happy atmosphere. Laughter can also make difficult situations easier to deal with by helping people see the funny side of things. However, many people laugh less in daily life because of stress from work, study or life. They are too busy or serious to find joy in small things. To laugh more, people can watch funny movies, read joke books, spend time with humorous people or even practice laughing for a few minutes every day. Doctors also suggest that people should laugh more to improve their physical and mental health. What is the writer's purpose of writing this passage?

options.A: To explain why laughter is good for physical and mental health.
options.B: To tell people laughter is beneficial but people laugh less, and suggest ways to laugh more.
options.C: To introduce the social benefits of laughter in strengthening relationships.
options.D: To advise people to watch funny movies and read joke books to laugh more.

exercises.explanation: 原文核心是“笑有诸多健康和社交益处，但现代人因压力笑的少，给出多笑的方法且医生建议多笑”。B选项全面概括写作目的；A选项仅提“笑的益处”，是原文一部分；C选项仅提“笑的社交益处”，是益处的细节；D选项仅提“多笑的具体方法”，是建议的细节，均不能作为写作目的。

lessons.title: 3-推断题解析
lessons.content:
### 推断题的核心定义
阅读理解推断题是中考英语阅读的**核心题型**（占比约20%-30%），考查学生根据文章已知的字面信息，通过逻辑分析、归纳、推理得出隐含意义的能力。推断题答案**不会直接出现在原文**，但必须基于原文信息，符合客观逻辑，绝对不能凭主观想象、常识或个人经验做题，是拉开阅读高分段差距的关键题型。

### 推断题的常见提问方式（中考高频）
1. 直接推断类：
   - What can we infer from the passage?
   - What does the writer probably want to tell us?
   - What can we learn about... from the text?
   - What is the writer's attitude towards...?
2. 隐含信息类：
   - Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?
   - What do we know about... that is not directly stated?
   - Why did... probably do...?（原文无直接原因）
   - What will the writer talk about next?
3. 观点态度类：
   - What is the writer's opinion on...?
   - How does the author feel about...?
   - The writer thinks that... is ______.

### 推断题的核心解题步骤（逻辑三步法）
#### 步骤1：定位原文——找“相关信息群”，而非单个句子
推断题的依据是原文的“信息集合”，而非孤立句子，需先锁定题干关键词对应的段落/多个句子，梳理这些信息的逻辑关系（因果、对比、递进、转折等）。
⚠️ 避坑：不要只看单个句子就下结论，需整合上下文信息。

#### 步骤2：逻辑推理——排除“显性信息+主观臆断”
- 第一步排除：直接摘抄原文的选项（推断题答案必是“隐含信息”，原文直接表述的内容可直接排除）；
- 第二步排除：不符合原文逻辑的主观推测（如原文说“他没带伞”，不能推断“他会淋雨”，可能有其他避雨方式）；
- 第三步推导：基于原文信息，通过“因果推导、对比分析、归纳总结”得出合理结论（如原文“超市晚上8点关门，他8:10到达超市门口”→可推断“他没能进入超市购物”）。

#### 步骤3：验证结论——符合“唯一逻辑+客观事实”
推断出的结论需满足两个条件：① 能被原文信息支撑（有且仅有这一个合理逻辑）；② 符合客观常识（但常识需服务于原文逻辑，不能替代原文）。对于“NOT INFERRED”类题目，需逐一验证选项，确保排除的选项要么是原文直接信息，要么是逻辑矛盾的推测。

### 推断题的高频解题技巧（针对不同推断类型）
#### 技巧1：因果推断——锁定“隐性因果”，推导未明说的原因/结果
原文常不直接表述完整因果，需通过上下文推导：
- 显性因果辅助：先找到because、so、as a result等信号词，再推导其隐含的延伸因果；
- 隐性因果核心：通过“行为+场景”推导因果（如“她把窗户关上，裹紧了外套”→推断“外面天气冷/有风”）。
⚠️ 避免“过度因果”：原文“他考试前没复习”→可推断“他可能考不好”，但不能推断“他一定考了倒数第一”。

#### 技巧2：对比推断——通过“已知对比项”推导“未知对比信息”
原文常给出两组对比对象的部分特征，需通过对比逻辑推导另一部分特征：
- 信号词：but、however、while、unlike、instead of；
- 推导逻辑：A和B对比，已知A的特征是X，则B的特征是非X（如“Tom likes outdoor activities, while his sister Lily is the opposite”→推断“Lily likes indoor activities”）。

#### 技巧3：态度/观点推断——通过“情感词+行为描述”推导作者/人物态度
态度不会直接说“我支持/反对”，需从细节中提炼：
- 正面态度：praise、happy、proud、thankful、support、satisfied（对应描述：smiled、nodded、positive words）；
- 负面态度：criticize、angry、disappointed、against、worried（对应描述：sighed、shook head、negative words）；
- 中立态度：objective、neutral、describe、introduce（无明显情感词，仅客观陈述）。

#### 技巧4：篇章延伸推断——根据“结尾内容”推导“下文走向”
中考常考“What will the writer talk about next?”，核心逻辑：
- 结尾提出新问题→下文大概率解决问题；
- 结尾介绍某事物的一个方面→下文大概率介绍该事物的其他方面；
- 结尾给出一个现象→下文大概率分析现象的原因/影响。
⚠️ 避免“无关延伸”：原文讲“城市交通拥堵”，结尾提“新能源汽车可能缓解拥堵”→下文应讲“新能源汽车如何缓解拥堵”，而非“新能源汽车的价格”。

#### 技巧5：数字/数据推断——通过“数据对比+趋势”推导隐含结论
原文给出数字/数据，但不直接总结，需通过数据分析推导：
- 数据对比：A公司利润从100万涨到200万，B公司从150万跌到50万→推断“A公司发展比B公司好”；
- 数据趋势：近5年城市共享单车使用率每年下降10%→推断“共享单车的市场需求在逐步降低”。

### 推断题的使用禁忌（避坑指南）
1. 把“原文直接信息”当推断答案：推断题答案一定是“没明说但能推出来”的内容，原文直接写的内容必错；
2. 主观臆断：加入原文没有的信息（如原文“他迟到了”，不能推断“他懒”，可能是堵车）；
3. 过度推断：从一个细节推导超出逻辑范围的结论（如原文“她买了机票”，不能推断“她要去国外旅游”，可能是国内行程）；
4. 用“常识”替代原文逻辑：即使选项符合常识，若原文无支撑信息，也不能选；
5. 忽略“转折词”：but、however后的内容是推断核心，易因忽略转折导致推理错误。

examples.sentence: For the past five years, a local bookstore in the city has been recording the number of books sold each month. From 2019 to 2021, the store sold an average of 5,000 books per month, with fiction books accounting for 60% of total sales. In 2022, the total monthly sales dropped to 4,000, and fiction books made up only 45% of sales. By 2023, monthly sales fell further to 3,000, and fiction book sales decreased to 30%. At the same time, the store began to sell more stationery and school supplies, which made up 25% of total sales in 2023, compared to only 5% in 2019. The store manager said, "We have to adapt to the changing needs of our customers to keep the store running."
examples.translation: 过去五年里，该市一家本地书店每月都会记录图书销量。2019至2021年，该店每月平均售出5000本书，其中小说类书籍占总销量的60%。2022年，月总销量降至4000本，小说类书籍仅占销量的45%。到2023年，月销量进一步降至3000本，小说类书籍销量占比下降到30%。与此同时，该店开始销售更多文具和学习用品，2023年这类商品占总销量的25%，而2019年仅占5%。书店经理说：“为了维持店铺运营，我们必须适应顾客不断变化的需求。”
examples.analysis: 这是典型的数字类推断题原文，无直接结论，需通过数据推导。若题干问“What can we infer about the bookstore from the passage?”，需整合数据逻辑：1. 2019-2023年图书总销量持续下降，小说类占比逐年降低→推断“小说类书籍的市场需求在该书店持续减少”；2. 文具占比从5%升至25%，经理提“适应顾客需求”→推断“书店增加文具销售是为了弥补图书销量下降带来的损失”。错误选项陷阱：① “The bookstore stopped selling fiction books in 2023”（原文是占比30%，不是停售，过度推断）；② “The bookstore made more money in 2023 than in 2019”（原文只有销量数据，无利润数据，主观臆断）；③ “Fiction books were the best-selling type in 2023”（2023年小说占比30%，文具25%，但其余45%未说明，无法推断，逻辑错误）。

examples.sentence: A research team did a survey on the daily screen time of teenagers aged 13-15 in a city. They surveyed 2,000 teenagers and found that 85% of them spend more than 4 hours on screens (including phones, computers and TVs) every day. Among those who spend over 6 hours on screens, 70% said they have trouble falling asleep at night, and 65% reported feeling tired during the day at school. Only 10% of teenagers who spend less than 2 hours on screens have sleep problems, and 90% of them said they can focus well in class. The researchers did not give any advice, but they mentioned that the survey results were "worrisome".
examples.translation: 一个研究团队对某市13-15岁青少年的每日屏幕使用时长进行了调查。他们调查了2000名青少年，发现85%的人每天花在屏幕（包括手机、电脑和电视）上的时间超过4小时。在每天屏幕使用时长超过6小时的青少年中，70%表示晚上难以入睡，65%反映白天在学校感到疲倦。在每天屏幕使用时长少于2小时的青少年中，只有10%有睡眠问题，90%表示能在课堂上集中注意力。研究人员未给出任何建议，但他们提到调查结果“令人担忧”。
examples.analysis: 该段落是调查类推断题原文，需通过数据对比推导隐含结论。若题干问“What can we infer from the research results?”，核心推导逻辑：1. 屏幕时长＞6小时的青少年，70%失眠+65%白天疲倦；屏幕时长＜2小时的青少年，仅10%失眠+90%注意力集中→推断“青少年每日屏幕使用时长过长可能导致睡眠问题和课堂注意力不集中”；2. 研究人员称结果“令人担忧”→推断“研究人员认为青少年过度使用屏幕是一个需要关注的问题”。错误选项陷阱：① “All teenagers who use screens for over 6 hours have sleep problems”（原文是70%，不是all，绝对化表述，过度推断）；② “The researchers will stop teenagers from using screens”（原文未提研究人员的后续行动，主观臆断）；③ “Screen time has no influence on students' study”（与原文数据矛盾，逻辑错误）；④ “85% of teenagers have sleep problems”（85%是屏幕时长＞4小时的比例，不是睡眠问题比例，偷换概念）。

examples.sentence: In a small town near the mountains, the local government has been trying to develop eco-tourism for three years. Before 2021, most people in the town made a living by cutting down trees and selling wood, and the mountain forests were getting smaller every year. In 2021, the government banned tree cutting and built hiking trails, camping areas and eco-friendly hotels. By 2023, more than 50,000 tourists visited the town every year, and many local people opened small restaurants or souvenir shops. The income of local families increased by 80% compared to 2020, and the mountain forests started to recover. A local farmer said, "We used to worry about making money, but now we know protecting the forest is better for us."
examples.translation: 在一个靠近山区的小镇，当地政府三年来一直试图发展生态旅游。2021年之前，镇上大多数人靠砍伐树木卖木材为生，山林面积每年都在减少。2021年，政府禁止砍伐树木，并修建了徒步小径、露营区和环保酒店。到2023年，每年有超过5万名游客到访该镇，许多当地人开了小餐馆或纪念品商店。当地家庭的收入比2020年增加了80%，山林也开始恢复。一位当地农民说：“我们过去担心赚钱的问题，但现在我们知道保护森林对我们更有利。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是场景类推断题原文，需通过行为和结果推导隐含信息。若题干问“What can we infer about the town's development?”，核心推导：1. 2021年前砍树为生→山林缩小；2021年后禁砍树+发展生态旅游→游客增加、收入涨80%、山林恢复→推断“发展生态旅游既保护了当地环境，又提高了居民收入”；2. 农民说“保护森林更有利”→推断“当地居民的环保意识和生计观念发生了转变”。错误选项陷阱：① “The government made all the money from eco-tourism”（原文是居民收入增加，不是政府赚所有钱，主观臆断）；② “No one in the town cuts trees anymore”（原文是政府禁止，未提是否所有人都遵守，过度推断）；③ “The mountain forests were completely recovered by 2023”（原文是“started to recover”，不是完全恢复，绝对化表述）；④ “Eco-tourism was the only way to make money in 2023”（原文提开餐馆、纪念品店，但未说“唯一”，过度推断）。

examples.sentence: A famous writer published a new novel in 2023. Before its release, the writer did not do any interviews or promotions, and the publisher only printed 10,000 copies at first. However, within two weeks of its release, all 10,000 copies were sold out, and the publisher had to print an additional 20,000 copies. Many readers left comments online saying that the novel's story was "close to real life" and "made them think about family and friendship". The writer's previous books had sold an average of 5,000 copies in the first two weeks, and none of them were reprinted. When asked about the success of the new novel, the writer said, "I just wrote what I saw and felt in daily life, not for making money or becoming famous."
examples.translation: 一位著名作家在2023年出版了一本新小说。在出版前，该作家未接受任何采访或进行宣传，出版商最初只印刷了1万册。然而，在出版后的两周内，1万册全部售罄，出版商不得不加印2万册。许多读者在网上留言称，这部小说的故事“贴近现实生活”，“让他们思考亲情和友情”。该作家之前的书籍在头两周平均仅售出5000册，且没有一本加印过。当被问及新小说的成功时，作家说：“我只是写下了我在日常生活中看到和感受到的东西，不是为了赚钱或成名。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是事件类推断题原文，需通过对比和细节推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the writer's new novel?”，核心推导：1. 新小说未宣传却两周售罄1万册+加印2万册，对比之前书籍头两周售5000册且不加印→推断“这部新小说比作家之前的作品更受读者欢迎”；2. 读者评价“贴近现实生活、引发对亲情友情的思考”→推断“小说的内容贴近生活是其受欢迎的重要原因”；3. 作家说“不是为了赚钱/成名”→推断“作家创作的初衷是表达生活感悟，而非追求名利”。错误选项陷阱：① “The writer became rich from the new novel”（原文无收入相关信息，主观臆断）；② “All readers loved the new novel”（原文是“many readers”，不是all，过度推断）；③ “The publisher knew the novel would be a success at first”（原文最初只印1万册，说明出版商最初未预料到成功，逻辑矛盾）；④ “The writer will stop writing after this novel”（原文未提后续计划，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: In a middle school, the school management team changed the lunch menu in 2023. Before the change, the lunch mainly included fried food, fast food and sweet drinks, and many students complained that the food was unhealthy and made them feel sleepy in the afternoon classes. After the change, the lunch included more vegetables, whole grains, fresh fruit and low-sugar drinks. The school also asked a nutritionist to design the menu to make sure it had enough protein and vitamins. After six months, the school did a survey among 1,200 students: 85% said they felt more energetic in the afternoon, 75% said they ate more vegetables than before, and only 10% complained about the lunch. The school principal said, "We care about students' health as much as their study."
examples.translation: 在一所中学，学校管理团队在2023年修改了午餐菜单。修改前，午餐主要包括油炸食品、快餐和含糖饮料，许多学生抱怨食物不健康，还让他们在下午的课上感到困倦。修改后，午餐包含更多蔬菜、全谷物、新鲜水果和低糖饮料。学校还请营养师设计菜单，确保菜单含有足够的蛋白质和维生素。六个月后，学校对1200名学生进行了调查：85%的学生表示下午更有精力，75%的学生表示比以前吃更多蔬菜，只有10%的学生抱怨午餐。学校校长说：“我们关心学生的健康，就像关心他们的学习一样。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是校园类推断题原文，需通过前后变化和调查结果推导隐含结论。若题干问“What can we infer from the passage about the lunch menu change?”，核心推导：1. 改前：油炸食品+快餐→学生困倦、抱怨；改后：健康饮食+营养师设计→85%学生更有精力、仅10%抱怨→推断“修改午餐菜单改善了学生的身体状态，提高了学生对午餐的满意度”；2. 校长说“关心健康如同关心学习”→推断“学校认为学生的健康状况会影响学习状态”。错误选项陷阱：① “All students felt energetic after the menu change”（原文是85%，不是all，过度推断）；② “The school will stop serving fried food forever”（原文是改后减少油炸食品，未提“永远停止”，过度推断）；③ “The nutritionist was a student's parent”（原文未提营养师身份，主观臆断）；④ “Students' grades improved after the menu change”（原文只提精力更充沛，无成绩相关信息，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A company that makes reusable water bottles started a campaign in 2023 to encourage people to use fewer plastic bottles. The company gave away 10,000 reusable bottles to people in five cities for free, and asked them to record how many plastic bottles they avoided using in three months. After three months, the participants reported that they used an average of 80 fewer plastic bottles per person. The company also found that in the five cities, the number of plastic bottles in the local garbage decreased by 15% compared to the same period last year. However, in other cities where the campaign was not held, the number of plastic bottles in garbage increased by 5%. The company CEO said, "Small changes in daily life can make a big difference to the environment."
examples.translation: 一家生产可重复使用水瓶的公司在2023年发起了一项活动，鼓励人们少用塑料瓶。该公司向五个城市的人们免费赠送了1万个可重复使用水瓶，并要求他们记录三个月内避免使用了多少个塑料瓶。三个月后，参与者报告称，每人平均少用80个塑料瓶。该公司还发现，这五个城市的本地垃圾中塑料瓶数量同比下降了15%。然而，在未开展该活动的其他城市，垃圾中的塑料瓶数量增加了5%。公司首席执行官说：“日常生活中的小改变能对环境产生大影响。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是环保类推断题原文，需通过对比和数据推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the campaign?”，核心推导：1. 活动城市塑料瓶垃圾降15%，非活动城市升5%→推断“该活动有效减少了塑料瓶的使用和丢弃”；2. 每人平均少用80个塑料瓶→推断“可重复使用水瓶的推广有助于减少塑料污染”；3. CEO说“小改变有大影响”→推断“公司认为个人的日常环保行为能汇聚成显著的环境效益”。错误选项陷阱：① “The campaign solved plastic pollution in the five cities”（原文是减少15%，不是解决，过度推断）；② “People in non-campaign cities refused to use reusable bottles”（原文未提非活动城市居民的态度，主观臆断）；③ “The company made no money from the campaign”（原文无盈利相关信息，主观臆断）；④ “80 plastic bottles per person is the national average”（原文是参与者的平均数据，不是全国平均，偷换概念）。

examples.sentence: In 2022, a museum in a big city started to offer virtual reality (VR) tours for visitors who could not come to the museum in person. Before the VR tours, the museum had an average of 500 visitors per day, mostly local people. After the VR tours were launched, the museum's online platform had 2,000 visits per day from people all over the country, and even some people from other countries. The museum also found that 60% of the VR visitors were young people under 30, while only 30% of the in-person visitors were under 30. The museum director said, "VR tours not only let more people see our exhibits, but also attract a younger audience to learn about history and culture."
examples.translation: 2022年，一座大城市的博物馆开始为无法亲自到访的游客提供虚拟现实（VR）参观服务。在推出VR参观前，该博物馆平均每天有500名游客，且大多是本地人。VR参观推出后，博物馆的在线平台每天有来自全国各地的2000次访问量，甚至还有一些来自其他国家的访问者。博物馆还发现，60%的VR参观者是30岁以下的年轻人，而线下参观者中只有30%是30岁以下。博物馆馆长说：“VR参观不仅让更多人看到我们的展品，还吸引了更年轻的观众来了解历史和文化。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是科技+文化类推断题原文，需通过数据对比和馆长话语推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the VR tours at the museum?”，核心推导：1. 线下日均500游客（多为本地人）→VR上线后日均2000次全国/海外访问→推断“VR参观扩大了博物馆的受众范围，打破了地域限制”；2. VR参观者60%是30岁以下，线下仅30%→推断“VR参观对年轻群体的吸引力远高于传统线下参观”；3. 馆长说“吸引年轻人了解历史文化”→推断“博物馆推出VR参观的目的之一是培养年轻人对历史文化的兴趣”。错误选项陷阱：① “The museum closed its in-person tours after VR tours”（原文未提关闭线下参观，逻辑矛盾）；② “All VR visitors were from foreign countries”（原文是“some from other countries”，不是all，过度推断）；③ “Young people don't like visiting museums in person”（原文是线下年轻参观者占比低，不是不喜欢，过度推断）；④ “VR tours made the museum the most popular in the country”（原文无对比其他博物馆的信息，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A study was done to find out the relationship between after-school activities and academic performance of middle school students. The study followed 800 students in Grade 8 for one year. Among the students who took part in at least one after-school activity (such as sports, music, art or reading clubs) for 2-3 hours every day, 80% got good grades (A or B) in the final exam. Among the students who did no after-school activities and spent all their free time doing homework, only 65% got good grades. The study also found that students in after-school activities had better communication skills and were more willing to help others. The researchers said, "After-school activities are not a waste of time; they help students develop in all aspects."
examples.translation: 一项研究旨在找出中学生课外活动与学业成绩之间的关系。该研究对800名八年级学生进行了为期一年的跟踪调查。在每天参加至少一项课外活动（如体育、音乐、美术或阅读俱乐部）2-3小时的学生中，80%在期末考试中取得了好成绩（A或B等级）。在不参加课外活动、所有空闲时间都用来做作业的学生中，只有65%取得了好成绩。研究还发现，参加课外活动的学生沟通能力更好，也更愿意帮助他人。研究人员说：“课外活动不是浪费时间；它们有助于学生全面发展。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是学业类推断题原文，需通过对比数据和研究人员话语推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer from the study results?”，核心推导：1. 参加课外活动的学生80%得好成绩，不参加的仅65%→推断“适度的课外活动不会影响学业成绩，反而可能有助于提高学业表现”；2. 参加课外活动的学生沟通能力更好、更乐于助人→推断“课外活动能培养学生的社交能力和品格”；3. 研究人员说“有助于全面发展”→推断“研究人员认为中学生应平衡学业和课外活动，而非只专注于作业”。错误选项陷阱：① “Doing more after-school activities means better grades”（原文是2-3小时，未提“越多越好”，过度推断）；② “Students who do homework all the time are bad at communication”（原文未提这类学生的沟通能力，主观臆断）；③ “All students in after-school activities got A grades”（原文是80%得A/B，不是all得A，过度推断）；④ “After-school activities are more important than homework”（原文是平衡，不是更重要，过度推断）。

examples.sentence: In a coastal city, the local weather station has noticed a change in the number of typhoons over the past 10 years. From 2013 to 2017, the city was hit by an average of 4 typhoons every year, and most of them were strong (level 10 or above). From 2018 to 2023, the city was hit by an average of 2 typhoons every year, but the number of heavy rains and floods increased by 30%. The weather station also found that the average temperature of the city increased by 1.2℃ in the past 10 years, and the sea level near the city rose by 5 cm. The chief meteorologist said, "The climate change is causing changes in the type of extreme weather we face, not just the number of typhoons."
examples.translation: 在一座沿海城市，当地气象站注意到过去10年台风数量的变化。2013至2017年，该市平均每年遭受4次台风袭击，且大多数是强台风（10级及以上）。2018至2023年，该市平均每年遭受2次台风袭击，但暴雨和洪水的次数增加了30%。气象站还发现，该市的平均气温在过去10年上升了1.2℃，城市附近的海平面上升了5厘米。首席气象学家说：“气候变化正在导致我们面临的极端天气类型发生变化，而不仅仅是台风数量的变化。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是气候类推断题原文，需通过多组数据和专家话语推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the coastal city's weather?”，核心推导：1. 台风数量减少，但暴雨/洪水增加+气温升1.2℃+海平面升5cm→推断“该市的气候正在发生变化，极端天气的类型从台风为主转向暴雨洪水为主”；2. 气象学家说“气候变化导致极端天气类型变化”→推断“气象学家认为气温上升和海平面上升可能是极端天气类型改变的原因”；3. 强台风数量减少但暴雨增加→推断“该市面临的气象灾害风险依然存在，只是形式发生了变化”。错误选项陷阱：① “The city will never be hit by typhoons again”（原文是平均2次/年，不是永不，过度推断）；② “The sea level rise is caused by heavy rains”（原文无因果关系，主观臆断）；③ “The temperature increase is good for the city”（原文未提气温上升的好处，逻辑矛盾）；④ “Floods are less dangerous than typhoons”（原文未对比灾害危险性，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A restaurant in a busy street changed its business model in 2023. Before the change, the restaurant only served lunch and dinner, with an average of 100 customers per day, and the average spending per customer was 80 yuan. The restaurant also had a high waste of food, with about 20% of the prepared food thrown away every day. After the change, the restaurant started to serve breakfast, and also launched a "small portion" option for all dishes. The restaurant also asked customers to order food according to their needs and gave a small discount to those who had no leftover food. After six months, the average number of customers per day increased to 150, the average spending per customer was 75 yuan, and food waste decreased to 5%. The restaurant owner said, "We wanted to reduce waste and also make the restaurant more welcoming to different customers."
examples.translation: 2023年，一条繁华街道上的一家餐馆改变了经营模式。改变前，该餐馆只提供午餐和晚餐，平均每天有100名顾客，人均消费80元。该餐馆还存在严重的食物浪费问题，每天约有20%的备餐被丢弃。改变后，餐馆开始提供早餐，还为所有菜品推出了“小份菜”选项。餐馆还要求顾客按需点餐，并给没有剩饭的顾客提供小额折扣。六个月后，平均每天的顾客数量增至150人，人均消费75元，食物浪费降至5%。餐馆老板说：“我们想减少浪费，同时让餐馆对不同的顾客更具吸引力。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是商业类推断题原文，需通过经营模式变化和结果推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the restaurant's business model change?”，核心推导：1. 改后顾客数+50人，人均消费-5元，但总营收（150×75=11250元）＞改前（100×80=8000元）+食物浪费从20%降至5%→推断“经营模式的改变既减少了食物浪费，又提高了餐馆的总营收”；2. 推出早餐+小份菜+剩饭折扣→推断“这些措施满足了不同顾客的需求（如吃早餐、少量点餐的顾客），吸引了更多客源”；3. 老板说“对不同顾客更具吸引力”→推断“餐馆改变模式的核心目标是兼顾环保和客源拓展”。错误选项陷阱：① “All customers ordered small portions after the change”（原文未提“所有顾客”，过度推断）；② “The restaurant made less money per customer so it lost profit”（总营收增加，逻辑矛盾）；③ “The discount for no leftover food was very big”（原文是“small discount”，不是big，偷换概念）；④ “The restaurant will stop serving dinner in the future”（原文未提，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: In 2022, a primary school in a rural area started a "book exchange" program for students. Before the program, most students in the school had only 2-3 books at home, and the school library had only 500 books, most of which were old. The book exchange program asked students to bring their own books to school and exchange them with classmates every week. The school also asked local families and businesses to donate books, and set up a new reading corner in each classroom. After one year, the average number of books per student at home increased to 8, and the school library had 2,000 new books. The school teacher said, "The program not only let students read more books, but also made them share and communicate with each other more."
examples.translation: 2022年，一所农村小学为学生启动了“图书交换”计划。计划实施前，该校大多数学生家里只有2-3本书，学校图书馆也只有500本书，且大多是旧书。图书交换计划要求学生每周带自己的书到学校与同学交换。学校还呼吁当地家庭和企业捐赠书籍，并在每个教室设立了新的阅读角。一年后，学生人均家庭藏书量增至8本，学校图书馆新增了2000本书。学校老师说：“该计划不仅让学生读了更多书，还让他们更多地分享和交流。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是教育类推断题原文，需通过计划前后变化推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the book exchange program?”，核心推导：1. 计划前人均藏书2-3本→计划后8本，图书馆新增2000本书→推断“图书交换计划有效增加了学生的阅读资源”；2. 学生交换书籍+教室设阅读角→老师说“更多分享和交流”→推断“该计划促进了学生之间的交流和分享意识”；3. 农村小学+呼吁捐赠书籍→推断“该学校之前面临阅读资源匮乏的问题，计划缓解了这一问题”。错误选项陷阱：① “All students had 8 books at home after the program”（原文是平均8本，不是所有，过度推断）；② “The local businesses donated all the new books”（原文是“asked families and businesses to donate”，未提“所有”，过度推断）；③ “The old books in the library were thrown away”（原文未提旧书处理，主观臆断）；④ “The program will be stopped next year”（原文未提，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A delivery company changed its delivery methods in 2023 to reduce carbon emissions. Before the change, the company used mostly gasoline-powered trucks for delivery, and the average carbon emissions per delivery were 1.5 kg. The company also had a high delivery delay rate, with 15% of deliveries arriving later than the promised time. After the change, the company used electric vehicles for short-distance deliveries and optimized the delivery routes to reduce unnecessary driving. The company also trained delivery drivers to plan their routes better and use navigation apps to avoid traffic jams. After six months, the average carbon emissions per delivery decreased to 0.8 kg, and the delivery delay rate dropped to 5%. The company manager said, "We wanted to be more environmentally friendly, and also improve our service quality."
examples.translation: 2023年，一家快递公司改变了配送方式以减少碳排放。改变前，该公司主要使用汽油动力卡车配送，每次配送的平均碳排放为1.5千克。该公司的配送延迟率也很高，15%的配送比承诺时间晚到。改变后，该公司使用电动车进行短途配送，并优化了配送路线以减少不必要的行驶。公司还培训配送司机更好地规划路线，并使用导航应用避免交通堵塞。六个月后，每次配送的平均碳排放降至0.8千克，配送延迟率降至5%。公司经理说：“我们希望更环保，同时也提高服务质量。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是物流类推断题原文，需通过方式变化和结果推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the delivery company's changes?”，核心推导：1. 改后碳排放从1.5kg→0.8kg，延迟率从15%→5%→推断“配送方式的改变既降低了碳排放，又提升了配送效率和服务质量”；2. 电动车+优化路线+司机培训→推断“这些措施是降低碳排放和延迟率的关键原因”；3. 经理说“兼顾环保和服务质量”→推断“公司认为环保措施和服务提升可以相辅相成，而非相互冲突”。错误选项陷阱：① “The company stopped using gasoline trucks completely”（原文是“mostly”改前用汽油车，改后短途用电动车，未提完全停用，过度推断）；② “All deliveries arrive on time now”（原文延迟率5%，不是全部准时，过度推断）；③ “The navigation apps were the only reason for lower delay rate”（原文是路线优化+司机培训+导航，不是唯一原因，过度推断）；④ “The company's profit decreased because of electric vehicles”（原文无利润相关信息，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A study on online shopping habits of people aged 40-60 was done in 2023. The study surveyed 1,500 people and found that 60% of them had shopped online at least once in the past month, compared to 40% in 2020. Among those who shopped online, 75% said they shopped for daily necessities (such as food, clothes and household items), while only 20% shopped online for daily necessities in 2020. 80% of the online shoppers said they found online shopping more convenient because they didn't need to go out, especially in bad weather. However, 45% of them said they were worried about the quality of the products and 30% had returned products because of poor quality. The researchers said, "Older people are accepting online shopping more, but there are still concerns that need to be addressed."
examples.translation: 2023年开展了一项针对40-60岁人群网购习惯的研究。该研究调查了1500人，发现60%的人在过去一个月内至少网购过一次，而2020年这一比例为40%。在网购人群中，75%的人表示网购日用品（如食品、衣物和家居用品），而2020年只有20%的人网购日用品。80%的网购者表示，他们觉得网购更方便，因为不需要出门，尤其是在恶劣天气下。然而，45%的人表示担心产品质量，30%的人因质量问题退货。研究人员说：“中老年人越来越接受网购，但仍有一些担忧需要解决。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是消费类推断题原文，需通过数据变化和观点推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about people aged 40-60 from the study?”，核心推导：1. 网购比例从40%→60%，日用品网购比例从20%→75%→推断“40-60岁人群的网购接受度显著提升，且网购品类从非日用品转向日用品，网购成为其日常消费的重要方式”；2. 80%觉得方便（不用出门）→推断“便利性是中老年人选择网购的主要原因”；3. 45%担心质量+30%退货→推断“产品质量问题是中老年人网购的主要顾虑”。错误选项陷阱：① “All people aged 40-60 shop online now”（原文是60%，不是all，过度推断）；② “Online shopping products are all of poor quality”（原文是30%退货，不是所有质量差，过度推断）；③ “Older people only shop online in bad weather”（原文是“especially in bad weather”，不是only，过度推断）；④ “The researchers will make online shopping laws”（原文未提研究人员的后续行动，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: In a community, the residents' committee started a "community garden" project in 2022. Before the project, the community had some empty land that was often full of garbage, and many residents complained about the environment. The project turned the empty land into a garden where residents could grow vegetables and flowers together. The committee also organized gardening classes for residents every month, and asked elderly residents to teach young people how to grow plants. After one year, the garbage in the empty land disappeared, and the community became greener. 90% of the residents said they felt closer to their neighbors, and 75% said they ate more fresh vegetables than before. The committee chairperson said, "The garden not only improved the environment, but also brought residents together."
examples.translation: 2022年，某社区的居民委员会启动了“社区花园”项目。项目实施前，该社区有一些闲置土地，这些土地经常堆满垃圾，许多居民抱怨环境问题。该项目将闲置土地改造成花园，居民可以一起种植蔬菜和花卉。居委会还每月为居民组织园艺课程，并请老年居民教年轻人如何种植植物。一年后，闲置土地上的垃圾消失了，社区变得更绿了。90%的居民表示，他们感觉与邻居的关系更亲密了，75%的居民表示比以前吃更多新鲜蔬菜。居委会主任说：“这个花园不仅改善了环境，还让居民们团结在了一起。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是社区类推断题原文，需通过项目前后变化推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the community garden project?”，核心推导：1. 闲置地从垃圾场→花园，社区变绿→推断“项目显著改善了社区的环境质量”；2. 90%居民觉得邻里关系更近+老年教青年园艺→推断“项目促进了社区居民之间的交流和代际互动”；3. 75%居民吃更多新鲜蔬菜→推断“居民自己种植蔬菜提升了其新鲜食材的获取量”；4. 主任说“兼顾环境和邻里团结”→推断“项目的双重目标都已实现”。错误选项陷阱：① “All residents grew vegetables in the garden”（原文是“could grow together”，不是所有居民都种，过度推断）；② “The community had no environmental problems after the project”（原文是垃圾消失、更绿，未提“无任何环境问题”，过度推断）；③ “The gardening classes were only for elderly people”（原文是为所有居民，老年教青年，逻辑矛盾）；④ “The garden made the community the best in the city”（原文无对比其他社区的信息，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A language learning app updated its functions in 2023. Before the update, the app only provided vocabulary and grammar lessons, and the average daily active users (DAU) were 500,000. The app also had a high user dropout rate, with 40% of users stopping using the app within one month. After the update, the app added interactive speaking exercises, real-life conversation scenarios and a community where users could practice speaking with each other. The app also gave personalized learning plans based on users' levels and goals. After three months, the DAU increased to 800,000, and the user dropout rate decreased to 15%. The app developer said, "We wanted to make language learning more practical and interactive, not just memorizing words."
examples.translation: 2023年，一款语言学习应用更新了功能。更新前，该应用仅提供词汇和语法课程，日均活跃用户（DAU）为50万。该应用的用户流失率也很高，40%的用户在一个月内停止使用。更新后，该应用增加了互动口语练习、真实生活对话场景，以及用户可以互相练习口语的社区。应用还根据用户的水平和目标提供个性化学习计划。三个月后，DAU增至80万，用户流失率降至15%。应用开发者说：“我们想让语言学习更实用、更具互动性，而不仅仅是背单词。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是APP类推断题原文，需通过功能更新和数据变化推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the app's update?”，核心推导：1. 功能更新（口语练习+对话场景+社区+个性化计划）→DAU+30万，流失率从40%→15%→推断“新增的互动和实用功能显著提升了用户活跃度和留存率”；2. 开发者说“不止背单词，更实用互动”→推断“之前用户流失的主要原因是学习方式单一（仅词汇语法），缺乏实用和互动性”；3. 个性化学习计划→推断“该功能满足了不同用户的学习需求，降低了流失率”。错误选项陷阱：① “The app will stop teaching vocabulary and grammar”（原文是新增功能，不是停止，逻辑矛盾）；② “All users liked the new functions”（原文流失率15%，说明有用户仍流失，不是所有喜欢，过度推断）；③ “The DAU will reach 1 million next month”（原文无后续预测数据，主观臆断）；④ “The speaking community was the only reason for higher DAU”（原文是多个功能，不是唯一原因，过度推断）。

examples.sentence: In 2022, a zoo in a big city changed its animal keeping methods. Before the change, most animals were kept in small cages, and visitors could only watch them from outside the cages. The zoo had an average of 2,000 visitors per day, and many visitors complained that the animals looked unhappy and inactive. After the change, the zoo built larger enclosures for animals that imitated their natural habitats (such as forests for tigers, rivers for penguins). The zoo also stopped selling animal feed to visitors to avoid overfeeding, and organized educational talks about animal protection every hour. After one year, the average number of visitors per day increased to 3,500, and visitors reported that the animals were more active. The zoo director said, "We want to let animals live in a better environment and also educate visitors about animal protection."
examples.translation: 2022年，一座大城市的动物园改变了动物饲养方式。改变前，大多数动物被关在小笼子里，游客只能在笼子外观看。该动物园平均每天有2000名游客，许多游客抱怨动物看起来不开心、不活跃。改变后，动物园为动物建造了更大的围栏，模仿它们的自然栖息地（如老虎的森林、企鹅的河流）。动物园还停止向游客出售动物饲料以避免过度喂食，并每小时组织一次关于动物保护的教育讲座。一年后，平均每天的游客数量增至3500人，游客反馈动物更活跃了。动物园园长说：“我们希望让动物生活在更好的环境中，同时也教育游客保护动物。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是动物园类推断题原文，需通过饲养方式变化和结果推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the zoo's changes?”，核心推导：1. 改后动物住仿自然栖息地+停止卖饲料→动物更活跃+游客从2000→3500→推断“改善动物居住环境提升了动物的生活状态，也吸引了更多游客”；2. 每小时组织动物保护讲座+园长说“教育游客”→推断“动物园的目标之一是提升游客的动物保护意识”；3. 游客之前抱怨动物不活跃，改后反馈更活跃→推断“游客更愿意参观动物生活状态良好的动物园”。错误选项陷阱：① “The animals were all happy after the change”（原文是“more active”，不是all happy，过度推断）；② “The zoo made no money from stopping feed sales”（原文无收入相关信息，主观臆断）；③ “Visitors can touch the animals now”（原文未提，主观臆断）；④ “The zoo will move all animals to the wild”（原文是仿自然栖息地，不是放归野外，过度推断）。

examples.sentence: A coffee shop near a university changed its opening hours and services in 2023. Before the change, the shop opened from 8 a.m. to 10 p.m., and mainly sold coffee and cakes. The average number of customers per day was 180, mostly students who came to buy coffee and leave. The shop also had few seats, so few students stayed to study. After the change, the shop opened from 6 a.m. to 12 p.m., added more tables and chairs, and provided free Wi-Fi and charging ports. The shop also launched a "study package" (coffee + snack + a quiet seat for 3 hours) at a discounted price. After four months, the average number of customers per day increased to 280, and 60% of customers stayed for more than 2 hours to study or work. The shop owner said, "We wanted to create a space that meets the needs of students, not just a place to buy coffee."
examples.translation: 2023年，一所大学附近的咖啡店改变了营业时间和服务。改变前，该店从早上8点开到晚上10点，主要售卖咖啡和蛋糕。平均每天有180名顾客，大多是买完咖啡就走的学生。该店座位也很少，因此很少有学生留下来学习。改变后，该店从早上6点开到晚上12点，增加了更多桌椅，还提供免费Wi-Fi和充电口。该店还推出了折扣价的“学习套餐”（咖啡+小吃+3小时安静座位）。四个月后，平均每天的顾客数量增至280人，60%的顾客会停留2小时以上学习或工作。店主说：“我们想打造一个满足学生需求的空间，而不仅仅是一个买咖啡的地方。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是商业+校园类推断题原文，需通过经营调整和结果推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the coffee shop's changes?”，核心推导：1. 调整营业时间（6点-12点）+增加座位+免费Wi-Fi/充电口+学习套餐→顾客从180→280，60%停留超2小时→推断“这些调整精准满足了学生的学习/工作需求，显著提升了客流量和顾客停留时长”；2. 之前顾客买完就走，现在多数停留学习→推断“咖啡店从单纯的消费场所转变为集消费和学习于一体的复合空间”；3. 店主说“满足学生需求”→推断“店主意识到学生群体的核心需求不仅是咖啡，还有学习空间”。错误选项陷阱：① “All students who came to the shop bought the study package”（原文未提“所有”，过度推断）；② “The shop made more money only from the study package”（原文是整体客流增加，不是仅套餐，过度推断）；③ “The shop will open 24 hours a day”（原文是6点-12点，未提24小时，主观臆断）；④ “No customers came to the shop before 8 a.m. after the change”（原文提前到6点开门，未提早8点前无顾客，逻辑矛盾）。

examples.sentence: A local government in a small city started a bike-sharing program in 2023 to reduce traffic jams. Before the program, the city's downtown area had serious traffic jams during rush hours (7-9 a.m. and 5-7 p.m.), and the average speed of cars was only 10 km/h. The government put 2,000 shared bikes in the downtown area and built more bike lanes. The government also encouraged people to ride bikes for short trips (less than 3 km) by giving a small reward to regular bike users. After six months, the average car speed during rush hours increased to 15 km/h, and the number of cars in the downtown area decreased by 20%. 70% of the bike users said they used to drive cars for short trips, and now they prefer riding bikes. The government official said, "Bike-sharing is a simple way to ease traffic pressure and promote green travel."
examples.translation: 2023年，一座小城市的当地政府启动了共享单车计划，以缓解交通拥堵。计划实施前，该市市中心在高峰时段（早上7-9点、晚上5-7点）交通拥堵严重，汽车平均时速仅10公里。政府在市中心投放了2000辆共享单车，并修建了更多自行车道。政府还通过给经常骑行的用户小额奖励，鼓励人们骑行共享单车进行短途出行（3公里以内）。六个月后，高峰时段的汽车平均时速增至15公里，市中心的汽车数量减少了20%。70%的共享单车用户表示，他们过去开车进行短途出行，现在更喜欢骑自行车。政府官员说：“共享单车是缓解交通压力、倡导绿色出行的简单方式。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是交通类推断题原文，需通过计划实施前后的数据推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the bike-sharing program?”，核心推导：1. 高峰时段车速从10→15km/h，汽车数量降20%→推断“共享单车计划有效缓解了市中心的交通拥堵”；2. 70%用户过去开车短途出行，现在骑单车→推断“共享单车计划成功引导部分市民从驾车短途出行转向绿色骑行”；3. 政府修更多自行车道+奖励骑行→推断“这些配套措施提升了市民骑行的意愿和便利性”。错误选项陷阱：① “There were no traffic jams in the downtown area after the program”（原文是车速提升，不是无拥堵，过度推断）；② “All car owners started riding bikes”（原文是20%汽车减少，不是所有车主，过度推断）；③ “The reward was the only reason people rode bikes”（原文是奖励+自行车道+缓解拥堵需求，不是唯一原因，过度推断）；④ “The bike-sharing program will expand to other cities”（原文未提，主观臆断）。

examples.sentence: A high school changed its exam system in 2023 to reduce students' stress. Before the change, students had monthly exams in all subjects, and the final grade was based only on exam scores. Many students said they felt stressed all the time, and some even stayed up late to study for exams. After the change, the school replaced monthly exams with monthly progress checks, which included not only written tests but also class participation, homework completion and project work. The final grade was based on 60% exam scores and 40% daily performance. After one year, the school did a survey: 85% of students said their stress levels decreased, 90% said they participated more in class, and 75% said they had more time for hobbies. The school principal said, "We want to evaluate students comprehensively, not just by their exam scores."
examples.translation: 2023年，一所高中改变了考试制度以减轻学生压力。改变前，学生所有科目都有月考，最终成绩仅基于考试分数。许多学生表示他们一直感到压力大，有些甚至熬夜备考。改变后，学校将月考改为月度进度检查，不仅包括笔试，还包括课堂参与、作业完成情况和项目作业。最终成绩由60%的考试分数和40%的日常表现构成。一年后，学校进行了调查：85%的学生表示压力水平降低，90%的学生表示课堂参与度提高，75%的学生表示有更多时间发展爱好。学校校长说：“我们希望全面评价学生，而不仅仅是通过考试分数。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是教育类推断题原文，需通过考试制度变化和调查结果推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the high school's exam system change?”，核心推导：1. 85%学生压力降低+90%课堂参与度提高+75%有更多爱好时间→推断“考试制度的改变有效减轻了学生压力，同时提升了课堂参与度，让学生有更多时间发展综合素质”；2. 成绩由60%考试+40%日常表现构成→推断“学校的评价体系从单一的分数导向转向综合能力导向”；3. 学生之前熬夜备考，现在压力降低→推断“新制度减少了学生的应试负担，更符合素质教育的要求”。错误选项陷阱：① “Students no longer need to take written tests”（原文是进度检查包含笔试，不是取消，逻辑矛盾）；② “All students got higher final grades after the change”（原文无成绩高低相关信息，主观臆断）；③ “The school will stop all exams in the future”（原文是改月考为进度检查，不是停止所有考试，过度推断）；④ “Class participation is more important than exam scores”（原文是40%日常vs60%考试，不是更重要，逻辑矛盾）。

examples.sentence: A bakery in a residential area changed its product range and sales methods in 2023. Before the change, the bakery only sold traditional bread and cakes, and most sales were from customers who came to the shop in person. The bakery had a low repeat customer rate, with only 30% of customers coming back more than once a week. After the change, the bakery added healthy products (such as whole-grain bread, sugar-free cakes) and customized cakes for special occasions (birthdays, festivals). The bakery also started online ordering and home delivery within 2 km. After five months, the repeat customer rate increased to 65%, and online sales accounted for 40% of total sales. The bakery owner said, "We wanted to meet the different needs of residents, from daily breakfast to special celebrations."
examples.translation: 2023年，一个居民区的面包店改变了产品种类和销售方式。改变前，该面包店只售卖传统面包和蛋糕，大部分销售额来自到店购买的顾客。该面包店的回头客率很低，只有30%的顾客每周光顾一次以上。改变后，面包店增加了健康产品（如全麦面包、无糖蛋糕）和为特殊场合（生日、节日）定制的蛋糕。面包店还开通了线上订购和2公里内的送货上门服务。五个月后，回头客率增至65%，线上销售额占总销售额的40%。面包店老板说：“我们想满足居民的不同需求，从日常早餐到特殊庆祝活动。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是餐饮类推断题原文，需通过产品和销售方式调整推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the bakery's changes?”，核心推导：1. 新增健康产品+定制蛋糕+线上配送→回头客率从30%→65%，线上销售占40%→推断“产品和销售方式的调整精准匹配了居民的多元化需求，显著提升了客户忠诚度和销售额来源”；2. 传统产品→新增健康产品→推断“面包店意识到居民对健康饮食的需求增加，调整产品结构以适应市场变化”；3. 线上配送+定制蛋糕→推断“这些措施提升了购买便利性，满足了居民的个性化和便捷化消费需求”。错误选项陷阱：① “The bakery stopped selling traditional bread and cakes”（原文是新增产品，不是停止，逻辑矛盾）；② “All customers ordered online after the change”（原文线上占40%，不是所有，过度推断）；③ “The healthy products were more expensive than traditional ones”（原文无价格相关信息，主观臆断）；④ “The bakery's delivery service covered the whole city”（原文是2公里内，不是全城，偷换概念）。

examples.sentence: In 2022, a library in a small town started a "mobile library" service for people who lived in remote villages. Before the service, the library was only in the town center, and people in remote villages had to travel 10-15 km to borrow books. Only 50 people from villages borrowed books from the library every month. The mobile library was a van with 2,000 books, and it visited each village once a week. The van also had a small reading area where villagers could read books on the spot. After one year, the number of village residents borrowing books increased to 300 per month, and 60% of them were children and elderly people. The library manager said, "The mobile library brings books to people who can't easily come to the library, and it also promotes reading in rural areas."
examples.translation: 2022年，一座小镇的图书馆为居住在偏远村庄的人们启动了“流动图书馆”服务。该服务推出前，图书馆只位于镇中心，偏远村庄的人们需要走10-15公里才能借书。每月只有50名村民从图书馆借书。流动图书馆是一辆装有2000本书的面包车，每周访问每个村庄一次。这辆面包车还有一个小型阅读区，村民可以当场阅读书籍。一年后，每月借书的村民数量增至300人，其中60%是儿童和老年人。图书馆馆长说：“流动图书馆把书带给那些不方便来图书馆的人，也促进了农村地区的阅读。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是文化类推断题原文，需通过服务推出前后的数据推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the mobile library service?”，核心推导：1. 每月借书村民从50→300人，60%是儿童和老人→推断“流动图书馆服务显著提升了农村居民的借书量，尤其满足了儿童和老年人的阅读需求”；2. 村民之前需走10-15公里借书，现在面包车上门→推断“服务解决了偏远村庄居民借书不便的问题，降低了阅读门槛”；3. 馆长说“促进农村阅读”→推断“流动图书馆是提升农村阅读率的有效方式”。错误选项陷阱：① “The mobile library had more books than the town library”（原文未对比藏书量，主观臆断）；② “All villagers in remote areas read books every week”（原文是借书量增加，不是所有村民每周读书，过度推断）；③ “The town library closed after the mobile library started”（原文未提关闭，逻辑矛盾）；④ “Children and elderly people didn't read books before the service”（原文是借书量增加，不是之前不读书，过度推断）。

examples.sentence: A fitness center near a residential area changed its membership system and class types in 2023. Before the change, the center only offered annual memberships (costing 2,000 yuan) and basic fitness classes (such as running and weight lifting). The average number of members was 300, and only 20% of members attended the fitness classes regularly. After the change, the center added monthly and quarterly memberships (costing 200 yuan/month and 500 yuan/quarter) and new classes (such as yoga, dance and low-intensity exercise for the elderly). The center also provided free fitness assessments for new members. After four months, the number of members increased to 500, and 60% of members attended classes regularly. 75% of the new members were women and elderly people, who rarely came to the center before. The center manager said, "We wanted to make fitness accessible to more people, not just young men who like intense exercise."
examples.translation: 2023年，一个居民区附近的健身中心改变了会员制度和课程类型。改变前，该中心只提供年卡（2000元）和基础健身课程（如跑步、举重）。平均会员数为300人，只有20%的会员定期参加健身课程。改变后，该中心新增了月卡和季卡（200元/月、500元/季），以及新课程（如瑜伽、舞蹈、针对老年人的低强度运动）。该中心还为新会员提供免费的体能评估。四个月后，会员数增至500人，60%的会员定期参加课程。75%的新会员是女性和老年人，这些人以前很少来该中心。中心经理说：“我们想让更多人能参与健身，而不仅仅是喜欢高强度运动的年轻男性。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是健身类推断题原文，需通过制度和课程调整推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the fitness center's changes?”，核心推导：1. 会员数从300→500，课程出勤率从20%→60%，75%新会员是女性/老人→推断“会员制度和课程类型的调整吸引了更多非传统健身人群（女性、老人），提升了会员数量和课程参与度”；2. 年卡→月/季卡（更低门槛）+低强度课程→推断“降低健身门槛、丰富课程类型是吸引新会员的关键”；3. 经理说“让更多人参与健身”→推断“中心的经营目标从服务小众群体转向覆盖更广泛的人群”。错误选项陷阱：① “The annual membership was stopped after the change”（原文是新增月/季卡，不是停止年卡，逻辑矛盾）；② “All new members were women and elderly people”（原文是75%，不是所有，过度推断）；③ “The fitness assessments made members healthier”（原文未提体能评估的效果，主观臆断）；④ “Intense exercise classes were removed”（原文是新增低强度课程，不是移除高强度，逻辑矛盾）。

examples.sentence: A toy company changed its product design and marketing strategies in 2023. Before the change, the company mainly made plastic toys for boys aged 6-10, and most sales were from physical stores. The company's sales growth rate was only 5% in 2022. After the change, the company made more wooden and eco-friendly toys, and designed toys for both boys and girls aged 3-12 (including educational toys for young children). The company also started selling toys on e-commerce platforms and made short videos to show how the toys could help children develop skills. In 2023, the sales growth rate increased to 18%, and online sales accounted for 50% of total sales. 60% of the new customers were parents who cared about eco-friendly and educational toys. The company CEO said, "We wanted to make toys that are not only fun but also beneficial to children's growth, and reach more parents."
examples.translation: 2023年，一家玩具公司改变了产品设计和营销策略。改变前，该公司主要为6-10岁男孩生产塑料玩具，大部分销售额来自实体店。2022年该公司的销售增长率仅为5%。改变后，该公司生产更多木制和环保玩具，并为3-12岁的男孩和女孩设计玩具（包括针对幼儿的益智玩具）。该公司还开始在电商平台销售玩具，并制作短视频展示玩具如何帮助孩子发展技能。2023年，销售增长率增至18%，线上销售额占总销售额的50%。60%的新客户是关注环保和益智玩具的家长。公司首席执行官说：“我们想制作不仅有趣，而且对孩子成长有益的玩具，并接触到更多家长。”
examples.analysis: 该段落是玩具行业类推断题原文，需通过产品和营销调整推导结论。若题干问“What can we infer about the toy company's changes?”，核心推导：1. 销售增长率从5%→18%，线上销售占50%→推断“产品设计和营销策略的调整显著提升了公司的销售增长和市场覆盖范围”；2. 塑料玩具→环保木制玩具，仅男孩→男女童，6-10岁→3-12岁+益智玩具→60%新客户是关注环保/益智的家长→推断“产品升级精准匹配了家长对环保、益智、全年龄段玩具的需求”；3. 实体店→电商+短视频营销→推断“线上渠道和内容营销帮助公司触达了更多目标客户”。错误选项陷阱：① “The company stopped making plastic toys completely”（原文是“more wooden/eco-friendly”，不是停止塑料玩具，过度推断）；② “All parents prefer eco-friendly toys now”（原文是60%新客户关注，不是所有家长，过度推断）；③ “Physical stores made no money after the change”（原文是线上占50%，不是实体店无收入，逻辑矛盾）；④ “The short videos were the only reason for higher sales”（原文是产品+营销双调整，不是唯一原因，过度推断）。

examples.sentence: When Sarah first moved to the new neighborhood, she was afraid to talk to her neighbors. She stayed at home every weekend and rarely went out. One day, her next-door neighbor, Mrs. Green, knocked on her door and gave her a plate of homemade cookies. Sarah was surprised and thanked her warmly. The next week, Mrs. Green invited Sarah to join the neighborhood garden club, where people planted flowers and vegetables together. Sarah hesitated at first, but she finally agreed. After a month, Sarah started to go to the garden club every Saturday, and she even began to chat with other neighbors when she walked her dog in the park. She no longer felt lonely and said, "This neighborhood is like a big family now."
examples.translation: 萨拉刚搬到新社区时，不敢和邻居说话。她每个周末都待在家里，很少出门。有一天，她的隔壁邻居格林太太敲了她的门，给了她一盘自制的饼干。萨拉很惊讶，热情地向她道谢。第二周，格林太太邀请萨拉加入社区园艺俱乐部，那里的人们一起种花种菜。萨拉起初有些犹豫，但最终还是答应了。一个月后，萨拉开始每周六去园艺俱乐部，她甚至在公园遛狗时开始和其他邻居聊天。她不再感到孤独，并且说：“这个社区现在就像一个大家庭。”
